config.title: Tränenpalast Berlin - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for tourists deciding whether to visit the free Palace of Tears museum at Friedrichstraße.

items.a: Yes, if you want a short, central and emotionally clear Cold War stop. **Tränenpalast Berlin** is especially strong for the human story of border crossings and farewells at Friedrichstraße.
Yes. The official Haus der Geschichte visitor information lists admission to Tränenpalast as free of charge.

items.q: How long do you need at Tränenpalast?
items.a: Most tourists should allow 45 to 75 minutes. You can go faster if you only want the main exhibition, or stay longer if you use the free audio guide carefully.

items.q: What are the opening hours for Tränenpalast Berlin?
items.a: The current official pattern is Tuesday to Friday 9:00-18:00, Saturday and Sunday 10:00-18:00, and Monday closed. Check the official page before leaving because holiday hours can differ.

items.q: Should I visit Tränenpalast or the Berlin Wall Memorial?
items.a: Choose Tränenpalast for the personal story of crossing and saying goodbye. Choose the Berlin Wall Memorial at Bernauer Strasse if you want the clearest explanation of the Wall as a border system.

items.q: Can I combine Tränenpalast with BerlinWalk?
items.a: Yes. BerlinWalk lasts about 2 hours through the historic centre, and Tränenpalast is a practical add-on before or after the tour if you still have time and energy.
